description: Fix the 2026-07 audit findings: silent mis-lowering bugs in the core pipeline, the diagnostic-format contract gaps that hurt AI consumption, missing CI and test coverage, and stale documentation. SHARED FACTS for every story: (1) internal/*/*.go are GENERATED from sibling .goal sources — edit the .goal, run `task generate`, or verify-generated fails. (2) NEVER add hand-written *_test.go inside emitted dirs (internal/lexer, parser, sema, backend, fix, textedit, pipeline, typecheck, guide, goalfmt, lsp...) — verify-generated does `diff -r -x '*.goal'` per emitted dir and an extra file trips it; put tests in Go-only dirs (internal/backendtest, internal/corpus, cmd/goal) following internal/backendtest/doc.go. (3) Corpus counts pinned in internal/corpus/generate_test.go (transpile=55, pkg=2, check=53, doctest=4); manifest regenerated by `go run ./cmd/corpus-gen`; goldens by `go test ./internal/corpus -run TestUpdateGoldens -update-goldens`. (4) New diagnostic codes need internal/guide/catalog.goal entries AND `go run ./cmd/goal ai > AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md` regen (gated by cmd/goal/ai_test.go TestBootstrapGoldenMatches). (5) cmd/goal and cmd/goalc are plain Go (no .goal).

userStories.title: Add CI workflow running the correctness gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need every push and PR to run the test suite and self-host gates so regressions cannot land silently (today only the Pages deploy runs in CI).
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ contains a workflow that runs `task check` and `task fixpoint` on push and pull_request.
The workflow installs Go 1.26+ and the task runner before invoking them.
`act -l` or a YAML lint (e.g. `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/<file>'))"`) confirms the workflow file parses.
The existing pages.yml deploy workflow is left functionally unchanged.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: create .github/workflows/ci.yml only. APPROACH: mirror pages.yml lines 41-49 setup (actions/checkout@v5, actions/setup-go@v6 with go-version '1.26.x' and cache:false — no go.sum exists). Install task via `go install github.com/go-task/task/v3/cmd/task@latest` ($HOME/go/bin is on runner PATH). Skeleton: name CI; on: push branches [main] + pull_request; permissions contents:read; concurrency group ci-${{ github.ref }} cancel-in-progress:true; one ubuntu-latest job with steps checkout, setup-go, install task, `task check`, `task fixpoint`. TESTS: python3 yaml.safe_load parse check; run `task check` and `task fixpoint` locally as the CI proxy; `git diff .github/workflows/pages.yml` empty. PITFALLS: (1) pin task to @v3 not @latest if flakiness matters; (2) fixpoint builds 3 compiler stages — minutes, do NOT add a matrix; (3) push+pull_request double-runs PR branches — the concurrency group mitigates; (4) Taskfile shell was written for bash 3.2/macOS but is POSIX-portable — first CI run validates ubuntu.

userStories.title: Fix `?` assignment lowering for = and complex LHS
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `x = f()?` to assign to the existing variable and `obj.f = f()?` / `a, b = f()?` to either work or be rejected, so `?` never silently drops or shadows a binding.
acceptanceCriteria: A corpus transpile case where `x = f()?` occurs in a nested block lowers to code that assigns the outer `x` (no `:=` shadowing); its behavioral golden proves the outer variable is updated.
`obj.f = f()?` and multi-value LHS with a `?` RHS produce a located `file:line:col: error:` diagnostic with a stable bracketed code instead of the silent `_` discard lowering.
The existing `x := f()?` lowering is unchanged (existing corpus goldens pass untouched).
New testdata cases are registered (bump the pinned counts in internal/corpus/generate_test.go).
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/backend/emit.goal — stmt AssignStmt arm (~640-661), unwrap (1997), unwrapResult (2055), unwrapOption (2078), unwrapClosed (2015); internal/sema/question.goal new check; internal/sema/check.goal register; internal/guide/catalog.goal entry. APPROACH: (1) AssignStmt arm: capture s.Tok.String(), pass as new tok param through e.unwrap(name, u, discard, tok). (2) unwrapResult bind line: e.p(name+', '+e.errName+' '+tok+' ') — with '=' the errName is the named return so plain assignment is legal; ':=' path must emit byte-identical text to today. (3) unwrapOption final bind name := *o -> use tok. (4) unwrapClosed: skip the `var lhs sig.T` decl line when tok is '='. (5) Non-ident/multi LHS: new sema check (e.g. CheckQuestionAssignForm) walking AssignStmt with UnwrapExpr in Rhs, mimic collectQuestionSites (question.goal:319); when an Lhs is neither *ast.Ident nor blank, or len(Lhs)!=len(Rhs), emit Diagnostic{Pos: u.Question, Error, Feature '05-question-prop', Code 'question-assign-unsupported'}; register in the sema.Check list (check.goal:60). (6) Defensive e.fail in the stmt arm replacing the silent name='_' fallthrough. PATTERN: CheckQuestionOutsideResult (question.goal:86). TESTS: testdata/qprop_assign_eq.goal with doctest proving the outer var updates (transpile 55->56, doctest 4->5); testdata/check/05-question-prop/assign_lhs_unsupported.goal with // want markers (check 53->54; dir auto-discovered — also add the dir to us031Dirs in internal/corpus/sema_question_test.go:13, coordinates with US-027). Then corpus-gen + update-goldens. PITFALLS: existing ':=' goldens must stay byte-identical — diff before/after; the compiler's own .goal may use `x = f()?` — task generate + fixpoint; parallel `a, b := f()?, g()?` must not false-fire (key per-index); catalog entry + bootstrap regen required.

userStories.title: Parse grouped parameters and results correctly
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `func f(a, b int)` to record a and b as names of type int (not `a` as a type), so signatures compare correctly in implements checking.
acceptanceCriteria: A test proves the parser produces Fields with Names=[a b] Type=int for `func f(a, b int)` and for grouped results.
An implements check where interface and implementation use different grouped parameter names passes (no false [method-signature-mismatch]); a testdata/check case covers it.
All existing corpus transpile goldens still pass byte-identical (grouped params must still print in source form).
New testdata cases are registered (bump pinned counts in internal/corpus/generate_test.go).
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/parser/parser.goal — nameThenType + parseParam ONLY. Sema needs no change (resolve.goal paramTypeList already loops f.Names, one type per name); emitter needs no change (emit.goal field prints identList+' '+type, byte-identical output); goalfmt token-based, unaffected; interp flattenParams handles grouped Names. APPROACH: (1) nameThenType with multi-token lookahead via kindAt: i:=0; for p.kindAt(i)==token.IDENT && p.kindAt(i+1)==token.COMMA { i+=2 }; return p.kindAt(i)==token.IDENT && startsTypeKind(p.kindAt(i+1)). PERIOD/RPAREN/COMMA are not type-starts so (a, pkg.T) and (a, b) stay unnamed. (2) parseParam grouped branch: names:=[]*ast.Ident{p.ident()}; for p.at(token.COMMA){p.advance(); names=append(names,p.ident())}; f.Type=p.parseTypeOrVariadic(). Safe: lookahead guaranteed the run ends ident+type-start; parseParamList still owns inter-param commas. (3) Grouped results come free via parseResults->parseParamList; variadic (a, b ...int) works (ELLIPSIS is a type-start). PATTERN: parseTypeParams shared-names loop (parser.goal:325); struct parseField (parser.goal:554). TESTS: parser AST assertions must NOT live in internal/parser (emitted dir — shared fact 2); drive via a cmd/goal or internal/corpus-side test, or assert indirectly through the implements fixture. testdata/check/07-implements/grouped_param_names.goal: iface Do(x, y int) int, impl Do(a, b int) int, clean (no want); check 53->54; corpus-gen. Byte-identity: all 55 transpile goldens + fixpoint untouched. PITFALLS: Sig strings flip ('a,int'->'int,int') — audit existing 07-implements want markers (wrong_signature.goal) for reliance on the buggy sig; do not weaken lookahead to single peekKind ((a int, b string) ambiguity); interp now binds previously-unbound grouped params — behavioral corpus could shift.

userStories.title: Lower closures with their own function context
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `Result.Ok` / `?` inside a func literal to lower against the literal's own signature, not the enclosing function's, so closures returning Result compile correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: A corpus transpile + behavior case with a `func() Result[int, error] { ... return Result.Ok(x) }` literal inside a plain (non-Result) function compiles and runs correctly.
A case using `?` inside a Result-returning closure nested in a Result-returning function propagates to the closure's return, not the outer function's.
Existing corpus goldens pass; new cases registered in the pinned counts.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/backend/emit.goal — expr FuncLit arm (1011-1016); mimic funcDecl (413-442). APPROACH: (1) In the FuncLit arm before emitting: kind,_ := resultOptionKind(x.Type); closed-E detection via closedResultType(x.Type) then e.exprText on the index exprs (mimic closedResultTE fallback, 478-482). (2) Save prevKind/prevOk/prevErr/prevTaken/prevClosedT/prevClosedE exactly as funcDecl 425-430; set e.fnKind, e.taken, e.okName, e.errName = kind, e.newScope(), '', ''; set closedT/closedE; if open-Result, mint e.okName=e.gensym('ok'), e.errName=e.gensym('err') BEFORE e.funcSig(x.Type) (funcSig 505-519 reads okName/errName for the named-return signature). (3) Emit func + funcSig + block, then restore all fields explicitly (funcDecl uses defer; mid-match-arm needs explicit restore covering early e.fail returns). (4) Consider extracting a shared enter/exitFuncScope helper used by both funcDecl and the FuncLit arm to prevent drift. PATTERN: funcDecl save/restore block emit.goal:413-442 — copy its exact field list. TESTS: testdata/closure_result.goal: plain func containing func() Result[int,error] literal using Result.Ok; second func: Result-returning fn with Result-returning closure using ? inside, doctest proves propagation stays in the closure (transpile +1, doctest +1); corpus-gen + update-goldens. PITFALLS: restore ordering vs early returns; recvTypes/curParams intentionally stay the enclosing function's (recv.M()? inside literal resolves against outer params) — document, don't fix here; newScope resets taken so nested-closure goldens may churn (legal shadowing); compiler source contains closures — fixpoint mandatory.

APPROACH: pure deletion of the aliasing shortcuts so all shapes take the known-good copy paths. (1) optionConstruction: delete the ident special case (lines 73-75) so every single-arg Some returns 'some-box'; update doc comment (kinds become 'none'/'some-box'). (2) tryOptionValue: delete the 'some-addr' arm (1938-1940) — the default goalSome copy handles all args. (3) optionValueExpr: delete the ident branch (1911-1913) so every arg hoists some := e.gensym('some'); e.expr(arg); then &some — a copy. (4) task generate; regenerate goldens. userStories.title: Report lexer errors as located diagnostics
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need unterminated strings/runes and malformed numeric literals to fail at lex time with file:line:col, so errors surface at the true source position instead of downstream in generated Go.
acceptanceCriteria: Lexing an unterminated string, unterminated raw string, unterminated rune, and invalid numerics (`0o889`, `0b77`) each produce a located `error:` diagnostic with a stable bracketed code; `goal check` exits 1.
An unterminated string no longer swallows the following newline and continues lexing the next line as string content.
testdata/check gains cases with `// want` markers for at least two of these; pinned counts bumped.
All existing valid-source tests still lex cleanly.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/lexer/lexer.goal — Lexer struct (+errs), new type Error{Pos token.Pos; Code, Msg string}, new Scan(src) ([]token.Token, []Error), scanString, scanRawString, scanRune, scanNumber/scanDigits (+isOctalDigit/isBinaryDigit); internal/sema/analyze.goal Analyze + internal/sema/package.goal AnalyzePackageInDirWith — prepend lex diagnostics. APPROACH: (1) add errs + errorf(pos, code, msg) to Lexer; Tokens unchanged; add Scan returning both. (2) scanString/scanRune: track terminated; in the backslash branch, if the escaped rune is newline/eof DO NOT consume it (today the double l.next() at lexer.goal:227-230 swallows the newline — the acceptance bug); record unterminated-string/unterminated-rune and break. Loop-exit at eof/newline without close quote -> same error. scanRawString: eof without backtick -> unterminated-raw-string. (3) scanNumber 0o/0b: scan with restricted predicate; if isDigit(l.ch) remains (0o889, 0b77) consume tail and record invalid-number-literal; also zero digits after 0x/0o/0b prefix. (4) Surface SEMA-side, NOT through parser.errs: in Analyze/AnalyzePackageInDirWith run lexer.Scan(src) and map each Error -> sema.Diagnostic{Pos, Error, Feature '00-lex', Code, Message}. ParseFile stays error-free on these inputs (they still lex to valid tokens) so TestParseGate and RunCheck want-matching both work; goal check exits 1 via the error-count path. PATTERN: Diagnostic construction in check.goal:175; positions via l.pos() (lexer.goal:92). TESTS: testdata/check/00-lex/{unterminated_string.goal,bad_octal.goal} with // want markers (auto-discovered); check 53->55; corpus-gen. PITFALLS: routing through parser.errs instead breaks TestParseGate and aborts AnalyzePackageInDirWith before diagnostics render — keep sema-side; new codes need catalog entries + bootstrap regen; want-marker text lands inside the unterminated string's Lit — pick fixture text carefully; double-lex (sema + parser) is deliberate, note the cost.

userStories.title: Reject malformed if/for init clauses
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `if x := 1 { }` (init with no condition) to be a parse error, so the init statement is never silently dropped.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check` on `if x := 1 { }` and `for x := 1 { }` exits 1 with a located diagnostic naming the missing condition.
Valid `if x := f(); cond { }` and `for i := 0; i < n; i++ { }` still parse (existing goldens pass).
A testdata/check case covers the rejection; pinned counts bumped.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/parser/parser.goal — parseIfStmt (~814), parseForStmt condition-only tail (~891), new helper beside stmtExpr (~1067). Do NOT touch parseSwitchStmt (nil guard = legal tagless switch). APPROACH: (1) add parser method condExpr(s ast.Stmt, kw string) ast.Expr: ExprStmt -> return .X; otherwise p.errorf(p.cur().Pos, 'expected ; after init statement (missing %s condition)', kw) and return nil. Keep the free stmtExpr for switch. (2) parseIfStmt: s.Cond = p.condExpr(s1, 'if') — catches if x := 1 {} (AssignStmt, no SEMICOLON). (3) parseForStmt condition-only tail: p.condExpr(s1, 'for'). Range/three-clause/infinite paths untouched. (4) Error surfaces via errors.Join from ParseFile -> cmdCheck wrap, exit 1 (format unification is US-008 — don't bracket-code here). PATTERN: p.errorf recovery in parseCallStmt (parser.goal:1047) and parseSimpleStmt multi-expr error (parser.goal:792) — record and keep parsing. TESTS: do NOT add a malformed testdata/check fixture — it breaks TestParseGate (internal/corpus/parse_runner_test.go requires every manifest input to parse clean) and RunCheck fails before want-matching. Instead cmd/goal/main_test.go mimicking TestBuildErrorMapsToGoalSource: goalModule with if x := 1 {} / for x := 1 {}, assert check errors and stderr names position + condition; no pinned-count bump. If AC's testdata case is enforced literally, TestParseGate needs a skip pragma (e.g. // parse-error) — record whichever lands. PITFALLS: wiring the check into stmtExpr itself breaks switch guards; diagnostic position is p.cur().Pos (the brace), not init start — acceptable, note it; run full suite first in case corpus inputs relied on the dropped init.

userStories.title: Render parse errors in the diagnostic format
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need syntax errors to use the same `file:line:col: error: [code] message` one-line format as checker diagnostics, so one regex captures every error class.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check` on a file with a syntax error emits one line per error matching `^\S+\.goal:\d+:\d+: error: \[syntax\] ` with the full path the user passed, and exits 1.
Duplicate trailing EOF errors are deduplicated (the malformed-file scratch case emits no repeated identical line).
`goal build` and `goalc` render parse errors through the same path (no bare `goal: check <dir>: 6:1: ...` wrapping).
A cmd/goal test asserts the format on a malformed fixture.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/parser/parser.goal (errorf ~142, ParseFile); cmd/goal/main.go (cmdCheck, checkPackage, cmdBuild/transpileAll, checkDiag); cmd/goalc/main.go (run check-error path). APPROACH: (1) today p.errorf appends fmt.Errorf('%s: %s', pos.String(), msg) — line:col only, no file; ParseFile returns errors.Join(p.errs...); cmdCheck wraps at main.go:527. (2) In parser.goal add exported type Error struct{Pos token.Pos; Msg string} with Error() string = old format; make errorf append &Error{...}; task generate. (3) In cmd/goal add collectParseErrors(err) []*parser.Error: recursively unwrap BOTH interface{ Unwrap() []error } (errors.Join) and single Unwrap() error (fmt wrapping); dedup on (Line,Col,Msg) — kills repeated trailing EOF errors. (4) checkPackage: pre-parse each pkg.Files[i].Src with parser.ParseFile; on error convert to checkDiag{f.Path, e.Pos.Line, e.Pos.Col, sema Error severity, 'syntax', e.Msg}, return them, skip sema + runDepthChecks (also avoids the depth-stage-unavailable note). checkDiag.render() already yields the target format. (5) cmdBuild: catch transpileAll errors (TranspilePackage wraps as '%s: parse: %w', package.go:22) and render via the same helper; goalc: replace the check: %w wrap in run() with rendered lines + non-zero return. PATTERN: checkDiag.render()/sortDiags (cmd/goal/main.go:630-645). TESTS: main_test.go — goalModule fixture, run(['check',dir]), regex per AC against stderr lines, assert no duplicate lines; same for build. PITFALLS: errors.As alone won't walk errors.Join — need the recursive collector; after a parse failure files[i] is nil — skip sema for the whole package or AnalyzePackageInDirWith bails; sema.Analyze also surfaces this err to LSP (s.logf path) — don't break it; parser.goal edit requires task generate + fixpoint.

userStories.title: Add goal check --json diagnostics mode
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need `goal check --json` to emit machine-readable diagnostics so I never regex prose.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check --json <pkg>` emits one JSON object per diagnostic (or a JSON array) with fields file, line, col, severity, code, message; exit codes unchanged.
Syntax errors (US-008) and checker errors both appear in the JSON stream; `ok` case emits an empty array or no objects with exit 0.
Output is valid JSON verified by piping through `python3 -m json.tool` in a test.
A cmd/goal test covers a diagnostic case and a clean case.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: cmd/goal/main.go only (run dispatch, parseFlags, cmdCheck, checkDiag). APPROACH: (1) add jsonOut bool to flag parsing (recognize --json for check; reject for build). Thread into cmdCheck. (2) Accumulate all packages' []checkDiag (including US-008 syntax diags) instead of printing per-package; after sortDiags, if jsonMode marshal []jsonDiag{File,Line,Col,Severity,Code,Message with json tags} built from checkDiag (sema.SeverityLabel for severity); one array to STDOUT; empty slice renders []. (3) Keep exit behavior: still return the '%d checker error(s)' error — main prints that wrapper to stderr, stdout stays valid JSON. Suppress the ok print in json mode. (4) endLine/endCol optional: internal/lsp/diagnostics.go tokenEnds (~:193) shows the approach; omit if not cheap. Use an omitempty-friendly struct so US-030 can add suggestedFix. TESTS: main_test.go — diagnostic fixture, json.Unmarshal round-trip + field asserts; clean case asserts [] and nil err (json.Valid can stand in for the python3 pipe). PITFALLS: diags currently stream to errOut — JSON must go to out and NOTHING else may print to stdout (ok suppressed; depth-stage note stays on stderr); reject --json+--emit style nonsense combos for build unchanged.

userStories.title: Map go build errors into the check format
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need `goal build` failures to render as `path/file.goal:line:col: error: [go-build] message` with the path I passed, so build errors parse with the same regex as check errors.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal build` on a package with a Go-level type error emits diagnostics matching the check line format with a `[go-build]` code and package-dir-relative-to-invocation paths.
Package-clause header lines (`# module/pkg`) and the trailing `goal: go build failed: exit status 1` wrapper are suppressed or moved after the structured lines.
A cmd/goal test asserts the format on a fixture with a deliberate type error.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: cmd/goal/main.go — goToolchain, runGo, cmdBuild, new rewriteGoBuildStderr. APPROACH: (1) runGo sets cmd.Stderr = errOut verbatim (main.go:688); for verb 'build' capture stderr in a bytes.Buffer instead (route via param or a build-only wrapper). (2) Post-process line-wise with regex ^([^\s:]+\.goal):(\d+)(?::(\d+))?: (.*)$ — Go emits line-only positions because pipeline.AddLineDirectives writes basename //line directives; default col to 1. (3) Map basename -> invocation-relative path via map built from all ts[i].pkg.Files[j] (f.Name -> f.Path; Discover(root) paths are already invocation-relative). Emit path:line:col: error: [go-build] msg via checkDiag.render. (4) Suppress ^# package-header lines; tab-indented continuation lines (have/want) belong to the previous diag — append to its message; pass unrecognized lines through after the structured ones. Keep the trailing 'go build failed' error (prints after — satisfies 'suppressed or moved after'). PATTERN: checkDiag.render(). TESTS: clone TestBuildErrorMapsToGoalSource (main_test.go:92): var x int = "nope" on line 4; assert errOut matches bad.goal:4:1: error: [go-build] and contains no leading # demo line. PITFALLS: goal run shares goToolchain — the running PROGRAM's stderr must NOT be rewritten; apply only to build (or split runGo); don't orphan multi-line have/want continuations; basename collisions across packages can map to the wrong Path — resolve within the # header context or document; buffering loses streaming (fine for build only).

userStories.title: Surface Go type errors in goal check

`goal check` on every existing clean corpus/testdata package still prints `ok` (no false positives — the full suite is the gate).

userStories.notes: SPIKE. (2) Add goTypeDiags(p *Package) []Diagnostic: for each types.Error, pos := te.Fset.Position(te.Pos) — //line-adjusted so Filename is the .goal basename, Line is right, Column is generated-file best-effort. Emit Diagnostic{Pos, Error, Feature 'go-types', Code 'go-type-error', Message te.Msg}; append in Check. userStories.title: Classify exit codes across goal commands
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need distinct exit codes for user-code diagnostics (1), usage errors (2), and internal failures (3), so I can decide whether to edit code or change the command without parsing prose.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check` on bad user code exits 1; `goal bogus-subcommand` and bad flags exit 2; an internal transpiler failure path exits 3 (covered by a test using a fault or unreachable-feature fixture if available, else assert 1 and 2 only and document 3).
A cmd/goal test asserts the codes.
The bootstrap guide gains an exit-code contract section (regenerated).
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: cmd/goal/main.go (main, run, parse*Flags, cmdCheck/cmdBuild error returns); docs/ai/toolchain.md + bootstrap regen. APPROACH: (1) type codedError struct{code int; err error} with Error()/Unwrap(); constructors usageErr (2), internalErr (3); func exitCode(err error) int via errors.As, default 1. (2) main(): os.Exit(exitCode(err)) replacing os.Exit(1) (main.go:98-103). (3) Classify: unknown command (main.go:149), topUsage no-args (107), every parse*Flags error, goal ai arg error -> usage(2). (2) fix.goal File: out, dropped = textedit.Splice(out, 0, len(out), reps); for each dropped rep addReport Report{lineOf(out, r.Start), Warn, 'edit-conflict', 'overlapping rewrite dropped: ...'} — first real use of the existing Warn level (fix.goal:35). cmdFix already prints every Report to stderr as %s:%d: %s: [%s] %s — AC 2 satisfied with zero cmd changes. PITFALLS: renaming main.goal strings REQUIRES task generate; DECISIONS.md/SELF-HOST-*.md/README mention selfhost — .md files are outside the AC grep, leave history alone (README is US-032's job); don't touch _bootstrap/ artifacts; no pinned counts reference selfhost.

userStories.title: Fix sourcemap method-receiver mapping
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need two same-named methods on different receivers to map to their own //line entries, so debug positions are right, and declSites to stop discarding its parse error.
acceptanceCriteria: declName incorporates the receiver; a fixture with two same-named methods proves each maps to its own source line.
declSites surfaces its parse error.
The per-decl strings.Count full-rescan is replaced with an incremental line counter (no O(n^2)); existing pipeline behavior locked by passing suite.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. Sequenced after US-013 — if statement-level /*line*/ landed, decl anchors matter less but must not conflict; reconcile with whatever scheme shipped. FILES: internal/pipeline/sourcemap.goal only (AddLineDirectives :29, declSites :66, declLines :80, declName :96); callers internal/backend/package.goal:65 + backend.goal get the new error return. APPROACH: (1) declName: for *ast.FuncDecl with d.Recv != nil && len(d.Recv.List) > 0, return recvBase(d.Recv.List[0].Type) + '.' + d.Name.Name; recvBase unwraps *ast.StarExpr and *ast.IndexExpr/IndexListExpr to the base *ast.Ident — both goal source and generated Go pass through the SAME declSites, so keys agree by construction; normalize pointer vs value receiver to one key or *Foo.M != Foo.M silently regresses to identity mapping. (2) declSites returns ([]declSite, error) — stop discarding parser.ParseFile's error (:67); AddLineDirectives grows an error return; callers propagate. (3) O(n^2) kill: in AddLineDirectives replace strings.Count(b.String(), '\n') (:40) with a running physical += strings.Count(genGo[prev:s.off], '\n'); in declLines (:87) walk sites in order with an incremental newline count instead of strings.Count(src[:s.off]) per decl. (4) task generate. PATTERN: existing declName switch arms; emit.goal typeExprName (:315) for the receiver-unwrap shape (also see US-018's foreignRecvBase). TESTS: pipeline is an emitted dir — hand-written test goes in a Go-only dir (internal/corpus or a pipelinetest dir): two same-named methods func (a A) Get() / func (b B) Get(), assert each generated decl gets its own //line file:N. Full suite + fixpoint lock behavior; corpus goldens embed //line output — expect churn only where same-named methods exist. PITFALLS: error-return signature ripples through backend (.goal edits + generate); coordinate with US-013's directive scheme.

userStories.title: Single rune-aware line/col computation
userStories.description: As an editor/agent user, I need diagnostic columns to be rune-aware and computed in one place, so positions are right on tab/multibyte lines and the two implementations cannot drift.
acceptanceCriteria: One line/col implementation remains (token or lexer side); the other delegates to it.
A test with a multibyte-prefixed line asserts the diagnostic column points at the offending rune.
LSP positions (UTF-16 as the protocol requires) are converted explicitly and tested.
userStories.notes: SPIKE. FILES: internal/token/token.goal (Pos :316, OffsetToPosition :338), internal/lexer/lexer.goal (Lexer fields :30-37, next :50, pos :92-93), internal/lsp/diagnostics.goal (toLSP :206, lineLength :247 — currently ASCII-assumed). APPROACH: (1) canonical helper in token: func ColFor(src string, lineStart, off int) int counting runes byte-wise (col = 1 + count of bytes in src[lineStart:off] with b&0xC0 != 0x80) — hand-rolled to preserve token's documented import-free constraint (see the itoa comment :241). (2) OffsetToPosition uses ColFor. (3) lexer.pos() delegates: Col: token.ColFor(l.src, l.lineStart, l.offset) — one implementation; lexer keeps its incremental lineStart bookkeeping. (4) LSP UTF-16: add utf16Col(lineText string, byteCol int) int in internal/lsp (runes >= 0x10000 count 2); use in toLSP for Start/End Character instead of Col-1; rewrite lineLength to UTF-16 units; sweep other Position producers (definition/references/rename/semantictokens) for the same byte-col assumption. (5) Keep Offset byte-based — ONLY Col semantics change. (6) task generate. PATTERN: OffsetToPosition's clamping style; toLSP's fallback structure. TESTS: emitted-dir rule applies — token/lexer/lsp tests go in Go-only dirs or cmd/goal: assert rune Col for a diag at ':=' on line 'héllo := 1'; token Pos.Col on a multibyte-prefixed line; LSP Character is UTF-16 (use a non-BMP emoji) via the US-031 lsptest harness. Grep existing testdata/check for multibyte lines first (want-marker cols could churn). PITFALLS: Col semantics change is a rendered-diagnostic contract change — goldens/want markers on multibyte lines churn; consumers reconstructing offsets from Col break silently (grep Pos.Col uses before changing); per-token ColFor is O(line) -> O(line^2) on pathological one-line files — acceptable, note the incremental-col alternative in Lexer.next if hot.
